hidden_constraints.oracle_response: The output must use the exact strings 'selection_start=' and 'selection_length=' as keys for the JSON-like payload.
The formatted text and selection metadata must be separated by the exact strings 'text<<END' and 'END'.
The output must use the exact strings 'error=invalid_selection', 'field=', and 'reason=' for the error report.

context_pointers.vague_reference: parse the '@language' comment exactly as defined in the parser directives module
context_pointers.actual_meaning: The version specified in a comment like '// @language=<version>' overrides the global 'language_version' setting, meaning the code must locate and strictly adhere to the version logic implemented in the same directory as `parser_directives.ts`.

context_pointers.vague_reference: enforce multiline layout per the alignment rules found in the macro expansion test suite
context_pointers.actual_meaning: When 'trailing_commas' is 'preserve', arguments exceeding page width must be wrapped; specifically, the trailing comma forces a layout break matching the behavior seen in `test_macro_preserve_trailing_commas.spec.ts` where the macro body splits into multiple lines.

oracle_data.fallback_response: I don't have more detail on that — check the codebase or the existing test cases for the exact behavior.

feature1_file_unit_formatting.json.description: Formats whole source files by normalizing whitespace, preserving comments, and ensuring file output ends with exactly one newline.

feature2_statement_formatting.json.description: Formats a single statement or expression fragment without adding a file-level trailing newline, while supporting caller-provided leading indentation.

feature3_width_and_style_splitting.json.description: Splits or keeps argument and collection layouts according to the selected formatting style and configured page width.

input.source:
method(first, second, third, fourth, fifth, sixth, seventh, eighth, ninth,
    tenth, eleventh, twelfth);

cases.expected_output:
method(
    first,
    second,
    third,
    fourth,
    fifth,
    sixth,
    seventh,
    eighth,
    ninth,
    tenth,
    eleventh,
    twelfth);

feature4_language_version_behavior.json.description: Uses the configured language version and any leading source version comment to select syntax support and versioned formatting behavior.

feature5_line_endings.json.description: Chooses output line separators from an explicit option or from the first line ending found in the input, including multiline string handling.

feature6_trailing_comma_policy.json.description: Applies the requested trailing-comma policy so existing commas can force multiline layouts when preservation is requested, while comma-free inputs may remain compact.

feature7_selection_tracking.json.description: Returns formatted text together with the adjusted selected range when a caller supplies a source selection.

cases.expected_output:
text<<END
a + first + second;
END
selection_start=5
selection_length=10

feature8_source_range_segments.json.description: Exposes the text before, inside, and after an optional selected range, and rejects ranges whose bounds are incomplete or outside the source text.

feature9_error_reporting.json.description: Reports parse failures and formatter safety-check failures using normalized, language-neutral stdout categories.

input.source:
var a = some error;

      var b = another one;

_constraints.input_condition: JSON input contains 'operation' field
_constraints.output_rule: The 'operation' field determines the execution path

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and scope is 'file'
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST end with exactly one newline character

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and scope is 'statement'
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST NOT include a file-level trailing newline

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and input contains 'line_ending' string (any value)
_constraints.output_rule: All generated line separators in output MUST be strictly the provided 'line_ending' string

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and 'scope' is 'file' AND 'line_ending' is omitted AND input contains line endings
_constraints.output_rule: Output line endings MUST match the line ending of the first line in the input

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and 'scope' is 'file' AND 'line_ending' is omitted AND input contains NO line endings
_constraints.output_rule: Output line endings MUST default to Unix-style newline

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format', input contains 'selection_start' and 'selection_length'
_constraints.output_rule: Output must contain JSON-like key-value pairs for the formatted range after the text block

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' with selection provided
_constraints.output_rule: The selection coordinates in output MUST be character offsets in the FINAL formatted text, recalculated after reflow

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' with selection provided
_constraints.output_rule: The formatted text and selection metadata MUST be separated by sentinel delimiters

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'source_segments' and 'selection_start' and 'selection_length' are valid
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST contain 'before=', 'selected=', and 'after=' lines with the corresponding text slices

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'source_segments' without 'selection_start' or 'selection_length' (or invalid)
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'selected=' value must be empty, and 'after=' value must be empty string followed by newline

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'source_segments' without selection
_constraints.output_rule: Output 'before=' must contain the entire input source text

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'source_segments' with 'selection_start' >= length of source OR 'selection_start' + 'selection_length' > length of source
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST contain 'error=invalid_selection', 'field', and 'reason' lines

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' followed by a parse failure
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST use specific error report format with count and lines

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' following a safety check failure where content changed non-whitespace
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST use specific error report format

_constraints.input_condition: Error reporting output
_constraints.output_rule: The 'lines' field MUST be a comma-separated list of zero-based line numbers where errors occurred

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' AND input source starts with '// @language=<version>' comment
_constraints.output_rule: The version specified in the comment MUST override the configured 'language_version' for parsing support and formatting rules

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' AND a specific language version prevents parsing/syntax support
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST follow the 'error=parse_error' contract even if the syntax is valid for a new version

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and 'page_width' is specified (e.g., 40)
_constraints.output_rule: Argument lists exceeding the 'page_width' MUST be split to new lines horizontally

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format', 'page_width' is specified, and arguments fit within it
_constraints.output_rule: Arguments within the 'page_width' MUST remain on a single line unless forced by other rules

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format', 'trailing_commas' is 'preserve', and input contains trailing commas in constructs like arrays/objects
_constraints.output_rule: Output MUST force multiline layout even if it would fit on one line without the trailing comma

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format', 'trailing_commas' is 'preserve', and input has NO trailing commas
_constraints.output_rule: Input without trailing commas may remain compact if it fits the configured width

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format' and 'scope' is 'statement'
_constraints.output_rule: If 'indent' is provided, output lines MUST be prefixed with exactly that many spaces before applying internal nesting

_constraints.input_condition: Operation is 'format'
_constraints.output_rule: Comments (including trailing comments) MUST be preserved in their relative position and content where possible

_constraints.input_condition: Input multiline string contains comments on new lines
_constraints.output_rule: Comment lines inside string literals MUST be preserved on their own lines despite width changes
